raw_consumer_statement:
I had purchased adjustable base, mattress, and pillows on XX/XX/year> in the amount of {$3000.00} and I had cancelled and refused delivery ( as per Tempur-Pedics advice ) for mattress and pillows ( shipped via UPS ) and the adjustable base was never delivered via XXXX. I do not have any of their merchandise of adjustable base ( {$1300.00} ), mattress ( {$1400.00} ), nor XXXX pillows ( {$170.00} ). I have only been credited {$1100.00} ( base minus home delivery fee times sales tax or ( {$1200.00} - {$170.00} ) XXXX = {$1100.00} ). I have followed their customer service representatives advice and refused delivery, and their website says that the mattress is returnable but they have not refunded for the return of the mattress nor pillow ( both were unopened as per their advice ). 

XXXX had provideXXXX as evidence of mattress and pillows being delivered but this was from my second order placed on XX/XX/year> after I spoke with their customer service representative assuring me that my first order was cancelled. I feel that this was a deliberate strategy, and not an honest mistake, by XXXX to confuse matters with Citibank and their XXXX party investigators. At this point, Citibank has concluded that I need to pay for both mattress and pillows, even though I had cancelled my order and their products have been returned to their respective warehouses.
